cover.title: THE CANDLE
cover.subtitle: The one whose warmth never changes — even when their heart quietly does.
cover.tagline: You treat everyone the same way. The one you love needs to feel that you don't.

profile_scores.caption: High Care · High Expression, Low Pride — Warm to everyone, but the deepening of your heart never quite shows up in how you express it

short_summary: For certain people, that warmth quietly turns into something else — genuine feeling.
You're clearly deeper in than before. But they still think, "they're just like that with everyone."

full_report.headline: THE CANDLE
full_report.intro: You're warm with everyone — friends, coworkers, even people you just met. This report follows what happens when that same warmth quietly turns into something deeper for one person: why the outside never changes even as the inside does, and what it takes for them to finally see the difference.

chapters.title: When You Love
body: You're warm with everyone.
Friends, coworkers, even people you just met.
You take care of them well. You speak warmly. You stay close.
So people watch you being warm and don't think much of it. "That's just who they are."
But here's the thing.
For certain people, that warmth quietly turns into something else — genuine feeling.
The problem is, what shows on the outside looks exactly the same.
You're clearly deeper in than before.
But they still think, "they're just like that with everyone."
chapters.profile_note: High Care · High Expression, Low Pride — Warm to everyone, but the deepening of your heart never quite shows up in how you express it

chapters.title: The Pattern You Keep Repeating — THE SAME TEMPERATURE CURVE
body: You're an attractive person at the start of a relationship.
Warm. Kind. Easy to be around.
They get drawn to you fast because of it.
The problem starts after that.
The "temperature" of your warmth stays nearly identical from the beginning to wherever you are now.
From their side, it just registers as "they're being just as good to me as always."
But your feelings keep getting deeper.
That gap keeps widening, until at some point you start wanting more.
You want to feel more serious. You want to become someone more special to them.
But because your way of expressing it never changed, they react with "what's gotten into you, suddenly?"
chapters.highlight: You're not hurt because there wasn't enough love. You're hurt because you showed the exact same temperature the entire time.

chart.title: THE SAME TEMPERATURE CURVE
chart.description: The warmth you express stays at the same temperature from start to finish, while your real feelings keep deepening underneath — until the gap finally breaks open at its widest point.

chapters.title: What You've Gotten Wrong About Yourself

items.belief: I'm warm to everyone, it's just my personality.
items.truth: That's true, to some extent. But not showing through expression that the intensity has changed behind that warmth isn't personality. It's habit. Your personality is warmth. Not distinguishing it is a learned pattern.
items.truth_short: That's true, to some extent. But not showing through expression that the intensity has changed behind that warmth isn't personality. It's habit. Your personality is warmth. Not distinguishing it is a learned pattern.

items.belief: If it's real, they'll figure it out eventually.
items.truth: No. Your baseline level of warmth is already so high that they have nothing to compare it against. You can't just wait to be figured out. You need to give a different kind of signal.
items.truth_short: No. Your baseline level of warmth is already so high that they have nothing to compare it against. You can't just wait to be figured out. You need to give a different kind of signal.

items.belief: If I treat them specially, they'll feel pressured.
items.truth: No. It's actually the opposite — not distinguishing at all is what confuses people more. Showing someone they're special isn't pressure. It's clarity.
items.truth_short: No. It's actually the opposite — not distinguishing at all is what confuses people more. Showing someone they're special isn't pressure. It's clarity.

chapters.title: The Person You Keep Choosing
body: Every face is different. But strangely, the people who come into your life have a similar texture.
People who just take your warmth as personality. No matter how well you treat them, it ends with "that's just how they are."
They never try to notice the feeling that's deepened underneath.
People who suspect you treat everyone else the same way too. They judge you by your usual behavior patterns rather than trusting your real feelings.
chapters.highlight: Someone who likes you receives the warmth. Someone who loves you notices that the temperature of that warmth has changed.

chapters.title: What Actually Changes Something
body: What you need isn't to get warmer. You've already got that covered.
What you actually need is to create one different behavior for just one person.
One small thing you don't do for anyone else, that you do only for them.
It doesn't need to be big.
A check-in you send only to them. A small habit you only do in front of them.
This isn't favoritism.
It's the signal that shows your feelings have actually deepened.
chapters.action_sentence: It's the signal that shows your feelings have actually deepened.

chapters.title: Where You Go From Here
body: You already know how to be warm to everyone. You know that very well.
What you're going to learn next is how to put levels into your warmth.
How to stay warm to everyone while showing a different temperature to one specific person.
This isn't about giving less warmth.
It's the opposite, actually.
It's about your real feelings getting across more accurately.
chapters.transition_note: This isn't about giving less warmth. It's about your real feelings getting across more accurately.

chapters.title: The Types You Keep Falling For

items.description: No matter how well you treat them, it's "that's just how they are." They never try to read the feeling that's deepened underneath.
They often ask, "you treat other people this well too, right?" They trust your usual pattern more than they trust your actual feelings.

items.type_title: The One Who Takes It for Granted
items.description: Barely says thank you. They assume your warmth will always just be there.

items.type_title: The One Who Ignores the Difference
items.description: You're taking care of them more than usual. But this person just reads it as "I guess they're in a good mood." They notice the signal, but they don't read what it means.

chapters.title: The Warning Signs You've Missed
chapters.intro_note: These signs actually showed up pretty early. It's just that you brushed them off with "that's just who I am."

signal_strength.label: They often ask, 'You're just as sweet to everyone else too, right?'
It stings when they say that, but you brush it off with 'that's just how I am'
Your feelings have clearly deepened, but the way you express them hasn't changed
They still take your warmth as your 'default setting'

chapters.core_signal: You already feel like this one person is special. They still see you as one of "the people who's warm to everyone."

chapters.title: What a Healthy Relationship Looks Like for You

checklist.title: They notice the difference in your warmth.
checklist.description: They feel it without you having to say it — that you're taking care of them more than usual.

checklist.title: They don't compare.
checklist.description: They don't ask "do you do this with other people too." They just trust what they feel.

checklist.title: They don't take your kindness for granted.
checklist.description: They say thank you for the small things too.

checklist.title: Being together makes you feel special too.
checklist.description: This is the important one. Not just always giving the warmth — being on the receiving end of it too, in this relationship.

chapters.title: What Happens In Your Next Relationship

sections.who_breaks_you: You almost never fail to express warmth. The problem is, it's always the same temperature. So there's a type that doesn't work for you: someone who just takes your warmth as "that's how they naturally are." They don't even try to distinguish it. So you keep showing the same temperature, and they never once notice.
sections.who_steadies_you: There's also someone who's a surprisingly good fit: someone who notices the subtle shifts in your warmth. Someone who says first, "I know you've been treating me a little differently lately." With someone like this, you don't have to spell everything out.
sections.same_pattern_repeats: You might be drawn again to someone who compares and doubts you. You might also be drawn to someone who takes your warmth for granted. That can't be stopped. That's just who you are. The issue was never the attraction itself — it's how long you keep showing the exact same temperature afterward.
sections.next_signs: Remember the signs from Chapter 8? They usually took months to show up. This time they'll show up faster, smaller. The moment you want to do something different just for them — right after that, there's an urge to lower it back down to your usual tone. That's the start.
sections.try_this: Normally you'd suppress that urge and go back to the same temperature. This time, don't. Just once. Go ahead and do that small thing you only do for them. And watch how they react. Someone who notices takes the difference naturally and gets closer. Someone who isn't right for you asks, "do you do that with other people too?" and tries to flatten it back to the same.

action_plan.summary: What you need isn't to get warmer. You need to create one different behavior for just one person.
action_items: Do one small thing you only do for them — a check-in, a habit, something you don't do for anyone else.
This time, don't lower it back down to your usual tone right after you feel the urge to make them special.
Watch whether they naturally notice the difference or try to flatten it back to the same.

closing.message: You've spent a long time learning how to be warm to everyone. The next report covers why your real feelings kept getting mistaken for ordinary kindness — and how to show the distinction more clearly.
closing.cross_sell_cta: Curious what your partner's pattern looks like? See their Cosmic Blueprint.
